A Wedding Guest's Guide  ·  Bhubaneswar

GV

Where the Temple City reveals itself — sacred stones, saffron skies, and the warmth of Odisha's hospitality.

Odisha  ·  India

Explore

Curated for Wedding Guests

Finest Dining in Bhubaneswar

From heirloom Odia thalis to rooftop lounges — a handpicked selection of the city's most beloved tables.

Atithi Devo Bhaba ↗ Map

Traditional Odia Thali · Saheed Nagar

The legendary Odia thali institution — Pakhala, Dalma, Chingri Patrapoda, stuffed pumpkin flowers. Four thalis easily feed seven. Non-Odias leave completely converted.

Must Try

Pakhala ThaliChingri PatrapodaDalmaKumra Phool Chingudi

📍 A/54, opposite Law University, Saheed Nagar  ·  Lunch & Dinner

Marigold Restaurant ↗ Map

Rooftop · Multi-Cuisine · Bapuji Nagar

Breezy rooftop with live in-house singers. Korean wings are a cult favourite. Go at night for the full vibe — the city looks beautiful from up here.

Must Try

Korean WingsCocktailsBiryaniGrills

📍 Hotel Marigold, Raj Mahal Square  ·  11 AM–11:30 PM

Moody Blues ↗ Map

Bar · Grills · Gajapati Nagar

Bhubaneswar's most popular evening destination. Well-designed interiors, the right music volume, food that justifies the price. Afghani kebab and LIIT are unmissable.

Must Try

Afghani Chicken KebabDal MakhaniLIITPaneer Tikka

📍 Triplex, Gajapati Nagar  ·  12 PM–11 PM

The BOMBAI ↗ Map

Retro Bollywood Café · Chandrasekharpur

A converted home turned retro-Bollywood café. Old switchboards, rooftop seating — feels like Café Mondegar in Mumbai. Exceptional coffee, unique in the city.

Must Try

Hot ChocolateCoffeeRooftop CocktailsGrills

📍 M-75, Samanta Vihar, Chandrasekharpur  ·  Tue–Sun 11 AM–11:45 PM

Lemon Grass ↗ Map

Chinese & Thai · Mayfair Lagoon Hotel

Bhubaneswar's finest Chinese inside the premium Mayfair Lagoon resort. Stuffed crab and Thai green curry are extraordinary. Serene lakeside setting.

Must Try

Stuffed CrabThai Green CurryBali PrawnsBaked Gur Rasogolla

📍 Mayfair Lagoon, 8-B Jayadev Vihar  ·  12–3 PM, 7–11 PM

Nakli Dhaba ↗ Map

North Indian · Dhaba Concept · Mayfair Lagoon

Mayfair's opulence dressed as a nostalgic roadside dhaba. Kashmiri Pulao with saffron and dry fruits is a revelation. Jhinga dum ki nisha is the showstopper.

Must Try

Kashmiri PulaoJhinga Dum Ki NishaDal MakhaniTandoori Grill

📍 Mayfair Lagoon, Jayadev Vihar  ·  12–3 PM, 7–11 PM

The Gourmet Restaurant ↗ Map

Multi-Cuisine · Swosti Premium Hotel

Bhubaneswar's highest-rated hotel restaurant. Impeccable buffet with Odia specialities, continental and North Indian options. Breakfast here is a full event.

Must Try

Odia Breakfast SpreadGrilled FishMalai Chicken Tikka

📍 Hotel Swosti Premium, Jayadev Vihar  ·  12:30–3:30 PM, 7:30–11 PM

Taste of China ↗ Map

Chinese · Saheed Nagar

The most loved mid-segment Chinese — 7,700+ Google ratings. E-Fu Noodles and Burnt Garlic Fried Rice are legendary. Budget-friendly and consistently excellent.

Must Try

E-Fu NoodlesManchow SoupBurnt Garlic Fried Rice

📍 625A, Janpath Road, Saheed Nagar  ·  12–10:30 PM

Kake Di Hatti ↗ Map

North Indian Veg · Khandagiri

Famous for its theatrical Aloo Naan made fresh on a live tandoor in front of you. Even dedicated non-vegetarians leave satisfied. The Kake Size naan is genuinely enormous.

Must Try

Aloo Naan (Giant!)Rajma MasalaKadhai Paneer

📍 Service Road, Khandagiri

Crunchy Munchie ↗ Map

All-Day Café · Laxmisagar

Beautifully designed all-day café. The Kolkata Mutton Biryani — tender meat, fragrant rice — is said to rival Arsalan. Great for both breakfast and a relaxed evening meal.

Must Try

Kolkata Mutton BiryaniKababsCappuccino

📍 Cuttack Road, Buddha Nagar  ·  7:30 AM–10:30 PM

Odia Sweets & Snacks Trail ↗ Map

Sweet Shops · Across the City

Chhena Poda from Cuttack Road sweet shops. Dahi Bara Aloo Dum from Old Town breakfast stalls. Gupchup everywhere. These are flavours no restaurant menu can replicate.

Must Try

Chhena PodaDahi Bara Aloo DumGupchupRasabali

📍 Old Town, Cuttack Road sweet shops, Puri Beach promenade

Temples · Caves · Beaches · Lakes

Sacred & Wondrous Odisha

The Temple City and its surroundings hold 2,000 years of devotion, carved stone, and coastline — here is where to begin.

TempleIn City

Lingaraj Temple

11th CenturyKalinga ArchitectureHindus Only

The spiritual soul of Bhubaneswar. This 1,000-year-old Shiva temple rises 55 metres in the Deul style of Kalinga architecture. Over 100 subsidiary shrines carry a devotional energy unlike any other.

Massive stone structures, detailed carvings, centuries of Shaiva worship. It makes you slow down naturally.

⏱ 6:30 AM – 7:30 PM  ·  Hindus only  ·  Free entry

TempleOld Town

Mukteswara Temple

950 CEGem of OdishaAll Welcome

Called the "Gem of Odishan Architecture." Its arched torana (gateway) is unlike any other in Indian temple architecture — small in scale but breathtaking in detail. Every stone face tells a story.

The decorated torana is the most beautiful stone arch in Odisha — sages and deities sculpted with extraordinary finesse.

⏱ 6:30 AM – 7:30 PM  ·  Old Town  ·  Free entry

TempleOld Town

Rajarani Temple

11th CenturyASI MonumentNo Deity

No presiding deity — purely an architectural pilgrimage. Famous for sculptural panels of celestial nayikas in red-gold sandstone that glows at golden hour. The walled gardens are meditative.

A quiet, walled garden of sculptural perfection — no rituals, no crowds, just art.

⏱ 6 AM – 9 PM  ·  Tankapani Road  ·  Nominal ASI entry fee

TempleOld Town

Ananta Basudev Temple

13th CenturyVaishnaviteBindusagar Lake

The only major Vaishnavite temple in a city of Shiva shrines, on the banks of sacred Bindusagar Lake. Mirrors Puri's Jagannath Temple in deity placement. Serves Mahaprasad daily.

Mahaprasad cooked in earthen pots on firewood — served in the temple courtyard in the afternoon. Divine.

⏱ 6 AM – 8 PM  ·  Bindu Sagar, Old Town  ·  Free entry

TempleNayapalli

ISKCON Temple Bhubaneswar

Lotus ArchitectureEvening AartiAll Welcome

A striking white marble temple shaped like a half-open lotus. The evening Aarti (6:30–7 PM) is electric and soul-stirring. The Bhojanalay serves pure vegetarian Mahaprasad.

Sitting in the main hall during Sankirtana, the vibrations are extraordinary. Spend 15 minutes in silence here — you'll carry it with you.

⏱ 4 AM – 8:30 PM  ·  NH-16, IRC Village  ·  Free entry

Caves8 km from City

Udayagiri & Khandagiri Caves

2nd Century BCEJain HeritageKing Kharavela

Twin hills carved into Jain monastic complexes under King Kharavela. Udayagiri's Rani Gumpha is a two-storey cave with elaborate carvings. The Hathi Gumpha bears Kharavela's autobiography in Brahmi script.

A king's autobiography carved into living rock 2,200 years ago — one of the most remarkable documents in India.

⏱ 6:30 AM – 7:30 PM  ·  Khandagiri  ·  ₹25 ASI entry

Buddhist Heritage8 km South

Dhauli Shanti Stupa

Emperor AshokaKalinga War SiteSound & Light Show

On this hill in 261 BCE, Ashoka witnessed the Kalinga War and renounced violence forever. The white Peace Pagoda overlooks the Daya River. Ashoka's original rock edicts are carved at the base.

The white dome stands against the sky like a beacon of non-violence. Below, the river flows where Ashoka's conscience changed the ancient world.

⏱ 6:30 AM – 6:30 PM  ·  Puri Road, ~8 km south  ·  Entry fee

Beach~60 km

Puri Golden Beach

Bay of BengalBlue Flag CertifiedSunrise Views

One of India's most iconic beaches — golden sand, sand sculptors, local food stalls, and the constant roar of the sea. The sunrise here, with temple spires in the distance, is unforgettable.

Devotees take ritual baths fifty metres from where children build sandcastles — Puri Beach is India in one frame.

⏱ Open all day  ·  Best at sunrise 5:30–6:30 AM  ·  ~60 km

Beach~65 km

Chandrabhaga Beach, Konark

Sun Temple NearbyQuiet & CleanSand Sculpting

3 km from the UNESCO World Heritage Konark Sun Temple. Far quieter and cleaner than Puri. A morning at Konark followed by this windswept coast is one of Odisha's great half-day outings.

When the evening light hits Konark's stone chariot wheels and you drive to this quiet beach — that is Odisha at its absolute finest.

⏱ Open all day  ·  Near Konark  ·  ~65 km from Bhubaneswar

Char Dham~60 km

Jagannath Temple, Puri

12th CenturyChar DhamHindus Only

One of India's four supreme Char Dham pilgrimage sites. The 12th-century complex rises 65 metres over Puri. The evening flag-change ceremony — a servitor climbing the spire without support — is witnessed to be believed.

The spiritual energy inside is unlike anywhere else. You don't observe the temple — the temple observes you.

⏱ 4:30 AM – 11:30 PM  ·  Hindus only  ·  Puri, ~60 km away

Lake & Wildlife~100 km

Chilika Lake

Ramsar WetlandIrrawaddy DolphinsMigratory Birds

Asia's largest coastal lagoon, spanning 1,100 sq km. Home to Irrawaddy dolphins, thousands of winter migratory birds from Siberia, and the sacred Kalijai Island temple in the middle of the water.

Watching flamingoes in flight against the golden hour sky over Chilika — this is the Odisha that stays with you forever.

⏱ Best Oct–Feb  ·  Barkul / Satapada entry  ·  ~100 km away

NatureIn City

Ekamra Kanan Gardens

Cactus HouseBoatingAnnual Flower Show

A sprawling botanical garden — an unexpected green lung in the city. Remarkable cactus collection, boating on the garden lake, and the annual flower show (February) transforms it into a riot of colour.

Bonsai, water fountains, thousands of blooms — a genuine hidden gem inside the city.

⏱ 6 AM – 8 PM  ·  Nayapalli, in city  ·  Nominal entry fee

UNESCO Heritage~65 km

Konark Sun Temple

13th CenturyUNESCO World HeritageKing Narasimhadeva I

One of the greatest architectural achievements of medieval India — a colossal stone chariot built for the Sun God Surya, with 24 intricately carved wheels and seven horses. The erotic and mythological sculptures on its walls are equal in artistry to Khajuraho. At dawn, the rising sun aligns perfectly through the temple's eastern gate.

The sheer scale of the chariot conception — carved from stone 800 years ago — makes every other monument feel smaller. Nothing prepares you for it.

⏱ 6 AM – 8 PM  ·  Konark, ~65 km  ·  ₹40 entry (Indians), ₹600 (foreigners)

Zoo & Nature15 km from City

Nandan Kanan Zoological Park

White TigersBotanical GardenBoating

One of India's finest zoos, set inside a lush forest reserve on the banks of Kanjia Lake. Nandan Kanan is famous for being the first zoo in India to breed white tigers in captivity. It also houses melanistic tigers, gharials, Indian pangolins, and a vast botanical garden. The cable car ride over the lake is a highlight for families.

The white tigers here are extraordinary. Walking through the forest paths feels more like a nature reserve than a zoo — genuinely peaceful and beautiful.

⏱ 8 AM – 5 PM  ·  Closed Mondays  ·  Barang, ~15 km  ·  ₹50 entry

Food Stop~20 km on NH-16

Pahala Rasagola Stalls

Odisha's Own RasagolaHighway StopSince 1930s

Long before the Bengal-Odisha rasagola debate became national news, the roadside sweet shops of Pahala village on NH-16 were serving what many consider the original — a softer, spongier, less sweet chhena ball soaked in light sugar syrup. Every family travelling between Bhubaneswar and Cuttack stops here. It is non-negotiable.

Pahala rasagola tastes different from anything you've had before — lighter, barely sweet, almost cloud-like. One piece is never enough. Neither is ten.

⏱ Open all day from ~7 AM  ·  NH-16, Pahala village  ·  ~20 km from Bhubaneswar  ·  ~₹10–15 per piece

Markets · Handicrafts · Street Eats

Shop & Savour Bhubaneswar

From hand-woven silks and Pattachitra scrolls to gupchup stalls and mishti counters — the city rewards those who explore on foot.

Handicraft MarketMust Visit

Ekamra Haat

Crafts Emporium · Unit 3, Bhubaneswar

The finest curated handicraft market in Odisha, run by the state government. Over 200 artisans sell directly — Pattachitra paintings, Sambalpuri silk, Dhokra metalwork, stone carvings, and palm-leaf manuscripts. Prices are fair and fixed. The best single stop for authentic Odia souvenirs.

Best Buys

Pattachitra PaintingsSambalpuri SilkDhokra FigurinesPalm-Leaf Art

📍 Unit 3, near Raj Bhavan  ·  10 AM–9 PM  ·  Closed Tuesdays

Utkalika

State Emporium · Rajmahal Square

The official Odisha government emporium — a reliable, no-bargaining destination for authentic handloom and handicrafts. The Sambalpuri and Bomkai sarees here are the real thing, with GI certification. Also stocks Kotpad organic cotton fabric, a rare and exquisite Adivasi weave.

Best Buys

Sambalpuri SareeBomkai SareeKotpad CottonPattachitra

📍 Rajmahal Square, Janpath  ·  10 AM–8 PM  ·  Mon–Sat

Shopping StreetBest Bargains

Janpath

Shopping Street · Central Bhubaneswar

Bhubaneswar's most popular shopping street — a long stretch lined with shops selling ready-made garments, Odia handloom fabrics, footwear, jewellery, and electronics. The footpaths are lined with vendors selling fashion accessories and street snacks side by side. Most shops are open late and bargaining is expected everywhere except branded stores.

Best For

Handloom FabricsFashion & ClothingFootwearStreet SnacksJewellery

📍 Janpath Road, Central Bhubaneswar  ·  10 AM–9:30 PM

Craft Village~30 km Away

Pipili Appliqué Village

Craft Village · Pipili Town, Puri Road

The village that gives the famous Pipili appliqué craft its name — vibrant cut-cloth work in crimson, saffron, and green stitched into canopies, lamp shades, bags, and wall hangings. Every shop in the main street is run by artisan families. Prices are a fraction of what you'd pay in city emporiums. Stop here en route to Puri.

Best Buys

Appliqué CanopiesLamp ShadesBags & PouchesWall Hangings

📍 Pipili Town, Puri Road  ·  ~30 km from Bhubaneswar  ·  All day

Dahi Bara Aloo Dum

Breakfast Street Food · Old Town & City-Wide

The undisputed king of Odia street food — soft vada (bara) soaked in tangy yogurt, topped with spiced potato curry (aloo dum), tamarind chutney, and sev. Eaten standing at roadside stalls, best before 10 AM when the bara is freshest. The Old Town stalls near Lingaraj are legendary for this.

Order With

Extra Tamarind ChutneyGreen ChilliChopped Onion

📍 Old Town near Lingaraj, Saheed Nagar stalls  ·  6:30–10:30 AM

Street FoodEvening Ritual

Gupchup (Pani Puri) Stalls

Street Food · Everywhere, All Day

Odisha's version of pani puri has a distinct character — the filling uses mashed potato with mustard and black chickpea, and the water (pani) is tangy-spicy with raw mango and cumin. Eaten in rapid succession, it is a competitive sport. The stalls outside Laxmisagar market and near Shaheed Nagar are the most popular in the city.

Ask For

Extra Spicy WaterSweet Meetha WaterAloo Filling

📍 Laxmisagar market, Saheed Nagar, Master Canteen Square  ·  4 PM–9 PM

Cuttack Road Sweet Shops

Mithai Shops · Cuttack Road

The stretch of sweet shops along Cuttack Road is the best place in the city for Chhena Poda — Odisha's baked cottage-cheese dessert, caramelised and slightly smoky on the outside. Also look for Rasabali (flattened chenna patties in condensed milk), Chenna Jhili (deep-fried cottage cheese soaked in syrup), and Arisha Pitha (rice flour jaggery pancakes).

Must Buy

Chhena PodaRasabaliChenna JhiliArisha Pitha

📍 Cuttack Road & Old Town sweet shops  ·  8 AM–9 PM

Esplanade One & Forum Mart

Shopping Malls · Rasulgarh & Chandrasekharpur

When the heat calls for an air-conditioned retreat, Esplanade One (Rasulgarh) and Forum Mart (Chandrasekharpur) are the two best malls in the city. Both have a good mix of Indian fashion brands, food courts with Odia snacks alongside fast food, and multiplex cinemas. Esplanade One is the larger and livelier of the two.

Good For

Indian Fashion BrandsFood CourtMultiplexAir Con!

📍 Esplanade One, Rasulgarh & Forum Mart, Chandrasekharpur  ·  10 AM–10 PM
